text:
The evidence is overwhelming and unambiguous. The automated checker's "metadata mismatch" is simply because it matched a *review* of the book published in the journal *Science* (authored by Mélanie Frappier), not the book itself. The actual book — with this exact title, these exact authors, and this exact publisher — is thoroughly verified across multiple authoritative sources.

EXPLANATION: The book *The Book of Why: The New Science of Cause and Effect* by Judea Pearl and Dana Mackenzie, published by Basic Books in 2018, is confirmed across Wikipedia, the ACM Digital Library, the authors' own UCLA page, Amazon, and Barnes & Noble. The automated checker's "mismatch" reflects it having matched a *Science* journal review of the book (by Mélanie Frappier), not the book itself.
LINK: https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.5555/3238230
FOUND_TITLE: The Book of Why: The New Science of Cause and Effect
FOUND_AUTHORS: Judea Pearl, Dana Mackenzie
FOUND_YEAR: 2018
VERDICT: UNLIKELY
